# Volaris

Volaris is a high-performance library for quantitative finance – option
pricing, volatility estimation, stochastic simulation, and numerical
methods, with a compiled C/C++ backend shared between Python and R.
Whether you are pricing derivatives, fitting volatility models, or
running Monte Carlo simulations, Volaris gives you C-speed without
leaving Python or R.

If you want a hands-on introduction to these topics, check out the
[notebooks](https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris/tree/main/notebooks)
directory for worked examples.

Available as:

- **Python package** – `volaris` (`python/`)
- **R package** – `Volaris` (`R/Volaris/`)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Features

- Pricing
  - Black-Scholes (with Greeks calculation)
  - Binomial Tree
  - Monte Carlo (European, Asian, Barrier)
  - Heston stochastic volatility
- Volatility
  - Historical volatility (Close-to-Close, Parkinson, Garman-Klass,
    Yang-Zhang)
  - Implied volatility
  - GARCH(1,1) fit & forecast
- Simulation
  - Geometric Brownian Motion (plain, antithetic)
  - Merton jump-diffusion
  - MCMC (Metropolis-Hastings)
- Numerical
  - Root-finding (Newton-Raphson, Bisection)
  - Numerical integration (Gauss quadrature, GSL adaptive)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Repository structure

    volaris/
    │
    ├── src/                            # Shared C/C++ source code
    │   ├── pricing/                    #   Black-Scholes, Binomial Tree, Heston, Monte Carlo
    │   ├── volatility/                 #   Historical vol, Implied vol, GARCH
    │   ├── simulation/                 #   GBM, Jump-Diffusion, MCMC
    │   ├── numerical/                  #   Root-finding, Integration
    │   └── utils/                      #   LCG random number generator
    │
    ├── python/                         # Python package
    │   ├── volaris/
    │   │   ├── _core.pyx               #   Cython wrapper (C modules)
    │   │   └── _heston_garch.pyx       #   Cython wrapper (C++ modules)
    │   ├── tests/
    │   └── pyproject.toml
    │
    ├── R/Volaris/                      # R package
    │   ├── R/                          #   R wrapper functions
    │   ├── src/                        #   C/C++ sources (copy from src/) + R wrappers
    │   │   ├── volaris_r.c             #     R/C API wrapper (C modules)
    │   │   └── heston_garch_rcpp.cpp   #     Rcpp wrapper (C++ modules)
    |   ├── man/                        #   Roxygen2-generated documentation
    |   ├── vignettes/                  #   Getting-started guide
    │   ├── tests/
    |   ├── _pkgdown.yml                #   pkgdown site configuration
    │   └── DESCRIPTION
    │
    ├── notebooks/                      # Jupyter notebooks with usage examples
    ├── .github/workflows/              # CI: R CMD check + pytest on Linux, macOS, Windows
    └── CHANGELOG.md

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Dependencies

**C/C++ backend (required for both packages)**

- [GNU GSL](https://www.gnu.org/software/gsl/) \>= 2.0: numerical
  integration
- OpenMP: parallelism in Monte Carlo and GARCH

**Python**

- Python \>= 3.9
- `NumPy` \>= 1.21
- Cython

**R**

- R \>= 4.0.0
- Rcpp

**Testing (optional)**

- Python: `pytest`
- R: `testthat`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Benchmarks

Benchmarked on Windows 11, Python 3.12, NumPy 2.x, and R 4.5.0.

**Python**

| Function | Volaris | NumPy / SciPy | Speedup |
|----|---:|---:|---:|
| `binomial_price` with N=400 | 0.1 ms | 3.9 ms | **36.6x** |
| `mc_price_european` with 100,000 paths | 3.5 ms | 6.3 ms | **1.8x** |
| `hist_vol_close_to_close` with n=10,000 | \<0.1 ms | 0.1 ms | **13.9x** |
| `implied_vol` x 10,000 calls | 12.0 ms | 25 933 ms | **2,169x** |
| `gbm_paths` with 10,000 paths and 252 steps | 56.3 ms | 146.9 ms | **2.6x** |
| `mh_sampler_gbm` with 10,000 iterations | 2.1 ms | 271.5 ms | **127x** |
| `rootfind_newton` x 10,000 calls | 12.2 ms | 1 893 ms | **155x** |
| `integrate_gauss` x 10,000 calls | 188.7 ms | 495.9 ms | **2.6x** |
| `garch_fit` with n=1,000 returns | 1.1 ms | 950.0 ms | **844x** |

**R**

| Function                                    |  Volaris |          R |   Speedup |
|---------------------------------------------|---------:|-----------:|----------:|
| `binomial_price` with N=400                 |  0.10 ms |    2.55 ms | **25.4x** |
| `mc_price_european` with 100,000 paths      |   5.2 ms |    19.3 ms |  **3.7x** |
| `vol_close_to_close` with n=10,000          |  0.03 ms |    0.13 ms |  **3.7x** |
| `implied_vol` x 10,000 calls                |  56.6 ms | 1 401.6 ms | **24.7x** |
| `gbm_paths` with 10,000 paths and 252 steps | 139.5 ms |   606.0 ms |  **4.3x** |
| `mh_sampler_gbm` with 10,000 iterations     |   9.2 ms |    87.4 ms |  **9.5x** |
| `rootfind_newton` x 10,000 calls            |  84.9 ms |   728.3 ms |  **8.6x** |
| `integrate_gauss` x 10,000 calls            | 120.5 ms |   351.6 ms |  **2.9x** |
| `garch_fit` with n=1,000 returns            |  10.6 ms |     1.6 ms |    *0.1x* |

*`garch_fit` is faster, because R 4.x compiles the inner loop on the
fly, making the sequential GARCH recurrence nearly as fast as C. The
sequential dependency prevents vectorisation in either implementation.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Usage examples

See the `notebooks/` directory for full worked examples:

- `notebooks/01_black_scholes.ipynb` – option pricing with Black-Scholes
  and a Binomial Tree
- `notebooks/02_implied_volatility_surface.ipynb` – volatility surface
  construction
- `notebooks/03_monte_carlo_pricing.ipynb` – Monte Carlo for pricing
  exotic options

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## License

MIT + file LICENSE

# Package index

## Pricing

- [`bs_price()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_price.md)
  : Black-Scholes option pricing
- [`bs_delta()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_delta.md)
  : Black-Scholes delta
- [`bs_gamma()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_gamma.md)
  : Black-Scholes gamma
- [`bs_vega()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_vega.md)
  : Black-Scholes vega
- [`bs_theta()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_theta.md)
  : Black-Scholes theta
- [`bs_rho()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/bs_rho.md)
  : Black-Scholes rho
- [`binomial_price()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/binomial_price.md)
  : Binomial tree options pricing
- [`mc_price_european()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/mc_price_european.md)
  : European options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation
- [`mc_price_asian()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/mc_price_asian.md)
  : Asian options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation
- [`mc_price_barrier()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/mc_price_barrier.md)
  : Knock-in / knock-out options pricing via Monte Carlo simulation
- [`heston_price()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/heston_price.md)
  : Heston stochastic volatility options pricing

## Volatility

- [`vol_close_to_close()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/vol_close_to_close.md)
  : Close-to-close historical volatility calculation
- [`vol_parkinson()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/vol_parkinson.md)
  : Parkinson volatility calculation
- [`vol_garman_klass()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/vol_garman_klass.md)
  : Garman-Klass historical volatility calculation
- [`vol_yang_zhang()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/vol_yang_zhang.md)
  : Yang-Zhang historical volatility calculation
- [`implied_vol()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/implied_vol.md)
  : Implied volatility (Black-Scholes inversion)
- [`garch_fit()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/garch_fit.md)
  : GARCH(1,1) parameter calibration
- [`garch_variances()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/garch_variances.md)
  : GARCH(1,1) historical conditional variances calculation
- [`garch_forecast()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/garch_forecast.md)
  : GARCH(1,1) conditional variances forecasting

## Simulation

- [`gbm_paths()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/gbm_paths.md)
  : Geometric Brownian Motion path simulation
- [`gbm_paths_antithetic()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/gbm_paths_antithetic.md)
  : Geometric Brownian Motion path simulation with antithetic variates
- [`merton_paths()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/merton_paths.md)
  : Merton jump-diffusion path simulation
- [`mh_sampler_gbm()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/mh_sampler_gbm.md)
  : GBM parameter calibration via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC

## Numerical

- [`rootfind_newton()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/rootfind_newton.md)
  : Newton-Raphson root finding
- [`rootfind_bisect()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/rootfind_bisect.md)
  : Bisection root finding
- [`integrate_gauss()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/integrate_gauss.md)
  : Gauss quadrature integratinon
- [`integrate_gsl()`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/integrate_gsl.md)
  : GSL QAGS adaptive integration

## Package

- [`Volaris-package`](https://allesgrau.github.io/volaris/reference/Volaris-package.md)
  : Volaris: High-performance library for quantitative finance
